Dear Kevin:
This wiII confirm our telephone conversation on May L4,
L99f. At that time, I advised you that we represent a recipient
committee that origj,nally organized to support and oppose ballot
measures at the city, county, and statewide l-evels, as indicated
on its Form 410 Statement of organization.
The connittee's initial 
activities v/ere to support referenda
sirnultaneously in a county election and a city election. 
The
committee filed a 21-day post campaign statement with respect to
these elections. 
Subsequently, the comrnittee has been supporting
a referendum in a different city. 
The committee expects to be
involved in additional different measures in the future.
My question to you was whether this committee needs to
continue fi,Iing 2l-day post carnpaign reports in each of these
j urisdictions?
You advised that the FPPC has consistently given advice that
such a committee is not considered as being primarily forned for
the purpose of supporting or opposing a measure, and therefore
should file as an ongoing general purpose comrnittee. The
committee srould not be required to file any further 2I-day post
campaign statements. In support of this advice, you provided me
srith a copy of a letter to charles c. Abdelnour, J.D., dated
Novenber, 199o (FPPC File Nunber A-90-670). Consistent $/ith this
advice, the comnittee that we represent should then plan to fil,e
regular serni-annual and/ ot pre-election campaign statements
pursuant to covernment Code S84211.
- Thank you for your assistance. I would appreciate it if you
s/ould please confirm this advice in writing.
Very truly yours,
2n,a

sections.question: My question to you was whether this committee needs to continue filing 21-day post campaign reports in each of these jurisdictions?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a recipient committee that supports and opposes ballot measures across multiple jurisdictions must file 21-day post-campaign statements for each jurisdiction or if it should instead file as an ongoing general purpose committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from counsel, not an FPPC response letter. The letter references prior oral advice from FPPC staff (Kevin) and requests written confirmation. The question and prior verbal conclusion are documented within the letter itself but this is a follow-up request, not FPPC's formal written advice response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: My question to you was whether this committee needs to continue filing 21-day post campaign reports in each of these jurisdictions?

embedding.summary: A law firm representing a recipient committee seeks written confirmation of prior telephone advice from FPPC staff regarding the committee's filing obligations when supporting multiple ballot measures across different jurisdictions.
